success_looks_like: I run /smoke-test and see a step-by-step report: triage PASS, checkpoint PASS, read PASS, ..., completion PASS
If a step fails (missing output field, wrong schema, step didn't advance), I see exactly what broke
After pipeline doc changes, I run this and know in ~5 minutes whether the pipeline still works
Test runs in isolation (branch or worktree) and cleans up after itself
Medium-weight test auto-approves at all gates except completion
failure_looks_like: The test is another cognitive walkthrough that just reads docs instead of actually running a directive
The test requires me to babysit each step manually
Test artifacts leak into the real repo
The test hangs at STOP gates with no way to auto-approve
dod.quality_bar: Running /smoke-test produces a clear pass/fail report per pipeline step within 5 minutes, with no manual intervention needed for medium-weight directives
examples: Like running `npm test` — one command, clear output, pass or fail
Like CI smoke tests — exercise the critical path, not every edge case

intent_version.reason: Add multi-weight support (lightweight + medium), worktree isolation, Option B gate simulation (full gate logic + default response)

iterations.reason: CEO: multi-weight was original requirement but incorrectly scoped out. Need worktree isolation instead of branch checkout, Option B for gate simulation (full gate logic + default response), concrete test cases per weight class, weight argument in run-smoke-test.sh
items: Option B test_mode in 04b-clarification.md and 07-plan-approval.md — full gate execution + default response
Worktree isolation in run-smoke-test.sh — replace branch checkout with git worktree add
Weight argument support — run-smoke-test.sh accepts lightweight/medium, SKILL.md passes $ARGUMENTS
Concrete test cases per weight — lightweight: create marker file; medium: create utility module with function + config

output.summary: Classified as medium — building a new skill with clear requirements, single domain, low risk.
output.rationale: New testing skill with prescribed approach. 2-3 tasks, framework-only changes.

output.summary: Fresh directive, no prior progress to resume.
Read directive.md. DOD extracted: 5 success criteria, 4 failure criteria, quality bar defined.
Read vision.md, lessons/orchestration.md, lessons/agent-behavior.md, lessons/skill-design.md. No preferences.md found.

Audited 5 scope areas. Skills are convention-based (.claude/skills/{name}/SKILL.md). validate-gate.sh can verify step artifacts. Recommended hybrid approach: spawn real /directive session with test_mode auto-approve. Branch isolation sufficient. 2 scenarios to migrate from lessons/scenarios.md.
Auto-approved for medium weight. Intent clear from CEO brief: real pipeline execution test, not cognitive walkthrough.

verified_intent.goal: Build a /smoke-test skill that runs a real directive through the pipeline and reports pass/fail per step
constraints: Must run a real directive, not simulate/read docs
Medium weight auto-approves at all gates
Clean up test artifacts on pass or fail
verified_intent.quality_bar: One command, clear pass/fail output within 5 minutes
acceptance_scenarios: CEO runs /smoke-test -> sees step-by-step pass/fail report
Pipeline doc change breaks a step -> smoke-test catches it with evidence
out_of_scope: Heavyweight test with pre-canned CEO responses

output.summary: EXTEND iteration: 1 delta project: smoke-test-multi-weight (P0, moderate). Devon builds, Sarah reviews. Worktree isolation + weight argument + Option B gate simulation + concrete test cases per weight.
Auto-approved for medium weight. Delta plan matches extend scope: 1 project, 4 tasks.

CTO decomposed smoke-test-multi-weight into 4 sequential tasks: worktree-isolation (replace branch with worktree), weight-argument-and-skip-sets (accept $1, skip sets per weight), gate-simulation-option-b (test_mode in 04b + 07), lightweight-test-directive (lightweight-specific content + validation). All moderate except task 4 (simple).
Reusing directive/enrich-agent-behaviors branch. No worktree needed for the directive itself (the smoke test creates its own worktree at runtime).
4/4 tasks completed (worktree-isolation, weight-argument-and-skip-sets, gate-simulation-option-b, lightweight-test-directive). All built by Devon, code-reviewed + reviewed by Sarah. All DOD met.
validate-reviews.sh passed for iteration 2 (smoke-test-multi-weight). All 4 tasks have build + review artifacts. No self-review violations. DOD verified by reviewer (Sarah) for all tasks.
Iteration 2 wrapup: digest updated with both iterations (7/7 tasks, 30 DOD criteria all met). Review-gate passed. No new follow-ups. Status set to awaiting_completion.
CEO approved completion. 2 projects, 7 tasks, 30 DOD criteria all met across 2 iterations (initial build + extend).
